name: unix-a-history-and-a-memoir
description: Brian Kernighan's memoir of Unix and Bell Labs — engineering principles, organizational lessons, and creative constraints behind one of the most influential software systems ever built.
useCase: Engineering culture design, fighting product complexity, building through constraints, tool investment decisions, knowledge work craftsmanship.
knownRisks: The book is a memoir from a participant's perspective — not a neutral history
Bell Labs was a unique institutional context that may not be replicable today
Some technical depth in the book requires basic programming knowledge
